prompt: I need a synchronous wrapper around an async client for legacy code. Right now it calls `asyncio.run()` inside methods, sometimes from threads, and sometimes while an app framework already has a running loop. Show the correct AnyIO-first boundary and explain when `BlockingPortal` is the right tool.
assertions: It says nested `asyncio.run()` under a running loop is invalid.
It distinguishes AnyIO worker threads from foreign threads.
It introduces `BlockingPortal` or token-based access for sync-to-async bridging.
It does not promise that cancelling the caller will stop arbitrary worker-thread code.
It names the references used and gives a verification step.
